title: Google pledges $40M in AI tools to DOE's Genesis Mission labs
summary: Google will supply DOE labs with AlphaFold 3, AlphaEvolve, WeatherNext and Gemini seats as its contribution to the US drive to double scientific discovery.
body:
Google and Google DeepMind announced on July 22 a $40 million commitment of AI tokens and cloud credits to the US Department of Energy's Genesis Mission, the national initiative launched by executive order last November that aims to double the pace of American scientific discovery within a decade.

## What's in the package

The commitment has two parts. Genesis Mission awardees get in-kind access to DeepMind's AI-for-science portfolio: AlphaEvolve, the Gemini-powered coding and algorithm-discovery agent; AlphaFold 3 for protein and biomolecular structure prediction; AlphaGenome for interpreting DNA variation; WeatherNext forecasting models; and AlphaEarth Foundations for planetary mapping. Separately, Google will fund one year of Gemini for Government seats and tokens for tens of thousands of users across the DOE national laboratory system.

The announcement leaned on early results from labs already using the tools. A Pacific Northwest National Laboratory researcher described using LLMs to automate the exploration of mathematical approaches, while another lab reported that embedding Gemini in scientific instruments cut electron-microscope calibration from over 90 minutes to about 13.

The pledge landed in the same week Washington committed $5 billion to AI-driven scientific research, and follows DeepMind's expanding collaboration with DOE announced alongside it.

Why it matters: the Genesis Mission is becoming the arena where frontier labs compete to be the default AI layer of American science, and in-kind donations of tools and tokens are the entry fee. For Google, seeding AlphaFold, AlphaEvolve and Gemini across 17 national laboratories buys long-term institutional lock-in that no marketing spend could match — and positions scientific discovery, rather than chatbots, as the public proof point for agentic AI. For researchers, the calculus is simpler: the tooling gap between AI-rich and AI-poor labs is about to widen fast.
title_zh: 谷歌向美国能源部"创世纪任务"注入 4000 万美元 AI 资源
summary_zh: 谷歌承诺向能源部国家实验室提供 AlphaFold 3、AlphaEvolve、WeatherNext 及 Gemini 政务版额度，助力美国十年内让科学发现提速一倍。
body_zh:
谷歌与 Google DeepMind 于 7 月 22 日宣布，向美国能源部"创世纪任务"（Genesis Mission）投入价值 4000 万美元的 AI token 与云额度。该任务由去年 11 月的行政令启动，目标是在十年内将美国科学发现的速度提高一倍。

## 资源包里有什么

这笔承诺分两部分。其一，"创世纪任务"的入选团队将获得 DeepMind 科学 AI 全家桶的实物使用权：基于 Gemini 的编码与算法发现 Agent AlphaEvolve、预测蛋白质及生物分子结构的 AlphaFold 3、解读 DNA 变异的 AlphaGenome、气象预报模型 WeatherNext，以及行星测绘基础模型 AlphaEarth Foundations。其二，谷歌将为能源部国家实验室体系内数以万计的用户提供为期一年的 Gemini 政务版席位和 token。

公告援引了已在使用这些工具的实验室的早期成果：太平洋西北国家实验室的研究员用大模型自动化探索数学思路；另一实验室称，把 Gemini 嵌入科学仪器后，电子显微镜校准时间从 90 多分钟压缩到约 13 分钟。

此番表态与华盛顿本周宣布的 50 亿美元 AI 科研投入同周落地，也延续了 DeepMind 与能源部不断加深的合作。

为什么重要："创世纪任务"正在变成前沿实验室争夺"美国科学默认 AI 层"的竞技场，而工具与 token 的实物捐赠就是入场券。对谷歌来说，把 AlphaFold、AlphaEvolve 和 Gemini 铺进 17 个国家实验室，换来的是任何营销预算都买不到的长期机构绑定，也让"科学发现"而非聊天机器人成为 Agent 型 AI 的公共示范场。对科研人员来说，账更简单：AI 富矿实验室与 AI 贫矿实验室之间的工具鸿沟，即将迅速拉大。

sources.title: Accelerating the frontiers of scientific discovery: Google's $40M commitment to the Genesis Mission
